user_outcome: The loop gates that consume a MODEL response accept schema-constrained structured output instead of scraping model prose in a model-specific format — so a capable model that does not reproduce the exact prose shape (as Grok did not) no longer fails the gate. Specifically the design-TL;DR gate (which required §1-§6 prose headers) and the reqverify Definition-of-Ready gate (which scraped a `## RESULTS` prose section) consume structured JSON validated against a schema; the schema is the contract, not the prose shape. This makes `sworn run` model-portable — the ADR-0011 structured-output keystone extended to the sites it missed.
rationale: Found by the 2026-07-11 dogfood: driving the loop with Grok, the design TL;DR was rejected ('model response missing required sections (need §1-§6 headers)' → Rule 2 deferral) and reqverify failed ('parsing model response: model response missing ## RESULTS section' → DoR blocked). Both scrape model PROSE in a format tuned to the models the gates were built against (sonnet/deepseek). ADR-0011 (keystone/structured-outputs) already made the verifier/captain verdicts schema-constrained (verifier-verdict-v1); the design-TL;DR and reqverify gates were not migrated. This slice migrates them to the same StructuredOutput/ChatStructured path (internal/model StructuredOutput, schema-constrained), so grok — or any StructuredOutput-capable model — passes. Builds on S01 (spec.json reads) in T1.
in_scope: internal/design (design TL;DR generation): the model emits a schema-constrained structured object (the §1-§6 sections as JSON fields) via ChatStructured, and the gate validates the structure — not a prose §1-§6 header scrape; a model that returns valid structured content passes regardless of prose formatting

internal/reqverify (DoR gate): the reqverify model call emits a schema-constrained structured verdict (results as JSON fields) instead of a `## RESULTS` prose section scrape; parse the structured object, not prose

Define/vendor the two response schemas following the verifier-verdict-v1 precedent (do not fork under an existing $id; if a baton schema is needed, use the vendored one or add a sworn-local one with a clear $id)
Fallback/degradation: if a model cannot emit structured output (no StructuredOutput capability), the gate records a clear Rule 2 deferral (not a silent pass, not a hard prose-format fail)
Tests: a model stub returning valid structured output passes the design + reqverify gates; a stub returning prose-only (no structure) is handled as a declared deferral, not a crash
out_of_scope: The file-read conformance (spec.json) — S01 owns that
The already-migrated verifier/captain verdict path (verifier-verdict-v1, ADR-0011) — unchanged, it is the precedent
Re-tuning prose PROMPTS — the fix is consuming structured OUTPUT, not rewording prompts
Interpreter/orchestrator scrapers not on the loop's design/DoR critical path — noted for a later sweep if the audit finds them load-bearing, out of scope here (declared)

risks.description: A structured-output migration changes the design/reqverify gate behaviour subtly, regressing an existing (sonnet/deepseek) run that relied on the prose path.
risks.mitigation: Keep the acceptance semantics (what makes a design/DoR PASS) identical; only the TRANSPORT (prose scrape → structured object) changes. Adapt existing tests, do not rewrite them; a prior-model stub must still pass.

risks.description: A model without StructuredOutput capability (some subprocess/keyless drivers) can no longer satisfy the gate.
risks.mitigation: Fail-closed to a declared Rule 2 deferral with a clear message (capability absent), never a silent pass; this matches the driver-contract capability model (structured is a declared capability).

effort_complexity.rationale: Small surface (two gates) but the structured-output migration must preserve acceptance semantics exactly while swapping the transport, and interact correctly with the StructuredOutput capability model — depth over breadth. May split (design vs reqverify) at design_review if the schemas diverge. Re-assess.

acceptance_criteria.text: When the design-TL;DR gate runs with a model that returns a valid schema-constrained structured design object (the §1-§6 content as fields), it SHALL accept it regardless of prose header formatting — proven by a stub whose prose lacks literal §1-§6 headers but whose structured output is valid (the exact Grok failure now passes).

When the reqverify DoR gate runs with a model returning a valid structured results object, it SHALL parse the structured object and not a `## RESULTS` prose section — proven by a stub returning structured results with no `## RESULTS` prose (the Grok DoR failure now passes).

If a model cannot emit structured output for the design or reqverify gate (no StructuredOutput capability), the gate SHALL record a clear Rule 2 deferral naming the missing capability, never a silent pass and never a hard prose-format failure.

The acceptance semantics of both gates (what constitutes a passing design / passing DoR) SHALL be unchanged from the prose path — a prior-model stub that passed the prose gate still passes the structured gate — and `go test ./internal/design/... ./internal/reqverify/... ./internal/model/...` SHALL pass.
